---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero-shell}
::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-copy}
::: {.hero-mark}
Minhyun Lee
:::

# Minhyun Lee

::: {.hero-tagline}
Research Staff at Samsung Electronics AI Center focused on semiconductor data intelligence and layout pattern generation.
:::

I work on label-efficient methods for semiconductor-chip data, including misalignment prediction, data preprocessing, and layout pattern generation. The focus is on building practical industrial vision systems that stay reliable under limited supervision.

::: {.chip-row}
`Semiconductor Data`
`Misalignment Prediction`
`Label-Efficient Learning`
`Layout Pattern Generation`
`Diffusion / VLM / RL`
`Industrial Vision`
:::

[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&hl=ko){.btn-hero}
[GitHub](https://github.com/gaviotas){.btn-ghost}
:::

::: {.hero-visual}
![](assets/profile/portrait.jpeg){.hero-photo}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section-intro}
## Current Work
Two main threads define the current role: semiconductor data intelligence and layout pattern generation. Both combine industrial data, efficient learning, and generative modeling.
:::

::: {.grid .theme-grid}
::: {.card .theme-card}
::: {.card-number}
01
:::

### Semiconductor Data Intelligence

Misalignment prediction and label-efficient preprocessing for semiconductor-chip data, with an emphasis on industrial data quality, noisy labels, and robust learning.
:::

::: {.card .theme-card}
::: {.card-number}
02
:::

### Layout Pattern Generation

Benchmarking and generation methods for semiconductor layout patterns, including diffusion-, VLM-, and RL-based approaches for semiconductor design.
:::
:::

::: {.section-intro}
## Research Themes
The core research focus is still visual learning, but now centered on practical, industrial, and label-efficient problems that matter in real deployments.
:::

::: {.grid .theme-grid}
::: {.card .theme-card}
::: {.card-number}
01
:::

### Industrial Vision and Label Efficiency

Reducing annotation cost for semiconductor data while improving preprocessing, robustness, and downstream usefulness.
:::

::: {.card .theme-card}
::: {.card-number}
02
:::

### Scene Understanding

Representation learning for segmentation, localization, and scene understanding in complex visual settings.
:::

::: {.card .theme-card}
::: {.card-number}
03
:::

### Segmentation and Localization

Object localization and dense prediction under limited supervision, with an emphasis on practical pseudo-supervision.
:::
:::

::: {.section-intro}
## Selected Publications
A compact list of representative papers, grouped by type.
:::
::: {.grid .paper-grid}
::: {.paper-card}
### [Railroad is not a train](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:qjMakFHDy7sC)

Saliency as pseudo-pixel supervision for weakly supervised semantic segmentation.

CVPR 2021
:::

::: {.paper-card}
### [Threshold matters in WSSS](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:YsMSGLbcyi4C)

Robust activation manipulation for accurate segmentation under threshold sensitivity.

CVPR 2022
:::

::: {.paper-card}
### [Saliency as pseudo-pixel supervision](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:roLk4NBRz8UC)

An extended journal treatment of weakly and semi-supervised semantic segmentation.

PAMI 2023
:::
:::

::: {.section-intro}
## Experience
:::

::: {.timeline}
::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-meta}
Dec 2024 - Present
:::
::: {.timeline-body}
### Research Staff, AI Center

Samsung Electronics  
South Korea

Misalignment prediction and layout pattern generation for semiconductor-chip data, with emphasis on industrial preprocessing and label efficiency.
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-meta}
Oct 2024 - Dec 2024
:::
::: {.timeline-body}
### Research Staff, SAIT

Samsung Electronics  
Computer Vision Lab @ SAIT

Image generation research for Samsung Electronics.
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-meta}
Mar 2019 - Aug 2024
:::
::: {.timeline-body}
### PhD Student

Yonsei University  
Seoul, South Korea

Advisor: Prof. Hyunjung Shim  
Computer vision, deep learning, and self-supervised learning.
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-meta}
Jul 2023 - Dec 2023
:::
::: {.timeline-body}
### Research Internship

NAVER Cloud  
Bundang, South Korea

Mentors: Song Park, Byeongho Heo, Dongyoon Han  
Storage-efficient vision training and industrial-scale model development.
:::
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
::: {.timeline-meta}
Jan 2018 - Sep 2018
:::
::: {.timeline-body}
### Student Intern

Yonsei University  
Seoul, South Korea

Built software for an online learning platform.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section-intro}
## Education
Academic training that supports the current research direction.
:::

::: {.grid .theme-grid}
::: {.card .theme-card}
::: {.card-number}
2019-2024
:::

### M.S. and Ph.D. in Integrated Technology

Yonsei University  
Advisor: Prof. Hyunjung Shim  
Completed in Aug. 2024.
:::

::: {.card .theme-card}
::: {.card-number}
2012-2019
:::

### B.S. in Computer Science

Yonsei University  
Graduated 2nd of 79.
:::
:::

::: {.section-intro}
## Explore More
Detailed research directions, publication records, and a concise CV are available as separate pages.
:::

::: {.grid .theme-grid}
::: {.card .theme-card}
### [Research](pages/research.qmd)

Current themes, problem settings, and ongoing directions.
:::

::: {.card .theme-card}
### [Publications](pages/publications.qmd)

Selected papers and the full publication list imported from Google Scholar.
:::

::: {.card .theme-card}
### [CV](pages/cv.qmd)

Short-form academic profile with a direct link to the PDF resume.
:::
:::

::: {.section-intro}
## Selected Projects
Projects that show the broader research base behind the current work. They cover vision-language learning, efficient training, industrial inspection, and earlier label-efficient research.
:::

::: {.pub-groups}
::: {.pub-group}
### Vision and Language

Text-guided and open-vocabulary segmentation work that connects language supervision with dense visual understanding.

::: {.pub-item}
#### Referring Image Segmentation

Text-guided object segmentation with multi-modal learning and effective augmentation.
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### Open-Vocabulary Part Segmentation

Open-vocabulary part segmentation with fine-grained image-text alignment and multi-granularity reasoning.
:::
:::

::: {.pub-group}
### Efficient Training

Training methods that reduce memory or annotation cost while keeping segmentation and representation learning practical.

::: {.pub-item}
#### Storage-efficient Large-scale Vision Training

Storage-efficient training with unsupervised representations and token-aware augmentation.
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### Label-efficient Semantic Segmentation

Semantic segmentation under limited annotation budgets.
:::
:::

::: {.pub-group}
### Applied Research

Industrial tasks that translate the core vision methods into inspection and restoration settings.

::: {.pub-item}
#### Efficient Surface Defect Detection

Lightweight anomaly detection for industrial surface inspection under severe imbalance.
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### Image Completion for Occlusion Area Restoration

Image completion for occlusion restoration in multi-view video settings.
:::
:::

::: {.pub-group}
### Earlier Work

Foundational label-efficient work that established the dense prediction and localization direction.

::: {.pub-item}
#### Semi-supervised Facial Landmark Detection

Low-shot facial landmark detection with hybrid heatmaps.
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### Self-supervised Object Localization

Self-supervised object localization for salient object discovery.
:::
:::
:::

## Publications
A compact list of representative papers, grouped by type.
\* indicates equal contribution.

::: {.pub-groups}
::: {.pub-group}
### Conference Papers

::: {.pub-item}
#### [Railroad is not a train: Saliency as pseudo-pixel supervision for weakly supervised semantic segmentation](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:qjMakFHDy7sC)

Seungho Lee\*, **Minhyun Lee**\*, Jongwuk Lee, Hyunjung Shim | Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), 2021
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### [Threshold matters in wsss: Manipulating the activation for the robust and accurate segmentation model against thresholds](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:YsMSGLbcyi4C)

**Minhyun Lee**\*, Dongseob Kim\*, Hyunjung Shim | Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), 2022
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### [Psynet: Self-supervised approach to object localization using point symmetric transformation](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:2osOgNQ5qMEC)

Kyungjune Baek\*, **Minhyun Lee**\*, Hyunjung Shim | AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence, 2020
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### [Understanding Multi-Granularity for Open-Vocabulary Part Segmentation](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:UebtZRa9Y70C)

Jiho Choi, Seonho Lee, Seungho Lee, **Minhyun Lee**, Hyunjung Shim | Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), 2024
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### [SeiT++: Masked Token Modeling Improves Storage-efficient Training](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:hqOjcs7Dif8C)

**Minhyun Lee**\*, Song Park\*, Byeongho Heo, Dongyoon Han, Hyunjung Shim | European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV), 2024
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### [Fine-Grained Image-Text Correspondence with Cost Aggregation for Open-Vocabulary Part Segmentation](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:Zph67rFs4hoC)

Jiho Choi, Seonho Lee, **Minhyun Lee**, Seungho Lee, Hyunjung Shim | Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), 2025
:::
:::

::: {.pub-group}
### Journal Articles

::: {.pub-item}
#### [Saliency as pseudo-pixel supervision for weakly and semi-supervised semantic segmentation](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:roLk4NBRz8UC)

**Minhyun Lee**\*, Seungho Lee\*, Jongwuk Lee, Hyunjung Shim | IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (PAMI), 2023
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### [Hybridmatch: Semi-supervised facial landmark detection via hybrid heatmap representations](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:eQOLeE2rZwMC)

Seoungyoon Kang\*, **Minhyun Lee**\*, Minjae Kim, Hyunjung Shim | IEEE Access 11, 26125-26135, 2023
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### [MaskRIS: Semantic Distortion-aware Data Augmentation for Referring Image Segmentation](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:kNdYIx-mwKoC)

**Minhyun Lee**\*, Seungho Lee\*, Song Park, Dongyoon Han, Byeongho Heo, Hyunjung Shim | Transactions on Machine Learning Research (TMLR), 2025
:::
:::

::: {.pub-group}
### Preprints

::: {.pub-item}
#### [CoPatch: Zero-Shot Referring Image Segmentation by Leveraging Untapped Spatial Knowledge in CLIP](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:ULOm3_A8WrAC)

NM An, I Kang, **Minhyun Lee**, Hyunjung Shim | arXiv preprint arXiv:2509.23098, 2025
:::

::: {.pub-item}
#### [Rethinking Weakly Supervised Semantic Segmentation Utilizing Both Point and Image Labels](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:KlAtU1dfN6UC)

**Minhyun Lee**, Hwijeong Lee, Hyunjung Shim | Available at SSRN 5062471, 0
:::
:::

::: {.pub-group}
### Patents

::: {.pub-item}
#### [Weakly supervised semantic segmentation device and method based on pseudo-masks](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=2hUlCnQAAAAJ&pagesize=100&citation_for_view=2hUlCnQAAAAJ:LkGwnXOMwfcC)

Hyunjung Shim, Seungho Lee, **Minhyun Lee** | US Patent 11,798,171, 2023
:::
:::
:::
